sanskrit: श्रद्धावाँल्लभते ज्ञानं तत्परः संयतेन्द्रियः । ज्ञानं लब्ध्वा परां शान्तिमचिरेणाधिगच्छति ॥
hindiTranslation: जो जितेन्द्रिय तथा साधनपरायण है, ऐसा श्रद्धावान् मनुष्य ज्ञानको प्राप्त होता है और ज्ञानको प्राप्त होकर वह तत्काल परम शान्तिको प्राप्त हो जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: The person with trust, discipline, and controlled senses gains knowledge. Having gained knowledge, that person quickly reaches supreme peace.
essence: Trust, discipline, and restraint make room for peace.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Trust, steady effort, and sense control bring knowledge. Once knowledge arrives, deep peace comes quickly.
simpleInsight: You do not force clarity. You prepare yourself, and peace arrives with understanding.
detailedMeaning: Krishna is still speaking to Arjuna on the battlefield, where hesitation has frozen action. After saying knowledge burns karma, he now explains who actually receives that knowledge: the one who trusts, stays focused, and masters the senses.
modernRelevance: You are standing outside a hospital room, phone in hand, about to hear bad news. Panic will not help. Trust, steadiness, and self-control create the inner space where clear understanding can land.
translationLiteral: श्रद्धावान् (full of trust) / लभते (attains) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / तत्परः (devoted, intent) / संयतेन्द्रियः (with controlled senses) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / लब्ध्वा (having attained) / पराम् (supreme) / शान्तिम् (peace) / अचिरेण (quickly) / अधिगच्छति (reaches)
insight: It shifts the mind from demanding certainty first to cultivating the conditions that let clarity arrive.

quoteVariants.text: Trust opens knowledge. Knowledge opens peace.
Trust is the doorway; peace comes after knowledge.
